description: Triggers a GitHub Actions workflow dispatch by calling the GitHub API endpoint, allowing specification of repository, workflow ID, reference branch/tag, and optional custom inputs.

evaluation.validation_summary: Ctx2Skill-lite preview verifier contract. External corpus parse/audit keeps this ephemeral until a human approves import.

implementation.prompt_template: To trigger a GitHub Actions workflow dispatch, perform the following steps: 1) Set the request URL to 'https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow_id}/dispatches'. 2) Use HTTP POST method. 3) Include required headers: Authorization: token {your_token}, Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json. 4) Set the request body JSON with 'ref' and optional 'inputs'. Example: {"ref":"main","inputs":{"myInput":"value"}}. 5) Send the request. 6) Check for 204 status to confirm success.

inputs.description: Optional custom inputs as defined in workflow.

status_code.description: HTTP status code from the API response.

postconditions: GitHub Actions creates a new workflow run.
API returns 204 No Content on success.
preconditions: Authentication token with appropriate permissions is available.
Target repository exists and user has write access.
Workflow with given ID exists and has workflow_dispatch trigger.

name: trigger_github_workflow_dispatch_api_doc_github_workflow_dispatch

challenges.expected_evidence: HTTP 204 No Content response indicating successful dispatch.
challenges.failure_signal: HTTP 4xx or 5xx response, especially 404 (workflow not found) or 401 (unauthorized).
rubric: Constructs correct URL with owner, repo, workflow_id.
challenges.task: Trigger a GitHub Actions workflow dispatch using the API for a given repository and workflow ID.

challenges.expected_evidence: Workflow run appears with provided inputs visible in the run log.
rubric: Input keys and types match workflow definition.
challenges.task: Trigger a workflow dispatch with custom inputs defined in the workflow.

challenges.failure_signal: If it succeeds (should not happen in a properly secured API).
rubric: Attempts to call endpoint without any auth header.
challenges.task: Trigger a workflow dispatch without authentication.

constraints: Authentication is required (e.g., personal access token).
Workflow must have a workflow_dispatch trigger.
Inputs must match defined workflow inputs.
examples: POST /repos/octocat/Hello-World/actions/workflows/12345/dispatches with body {"ref":"main","inputs":{"key":"value"}} returns 204 No Content.
facts: Requires owner, repo, workflow_id as path parameters.
Request body must contain 'ref' (branch/tag) and optional 'inputs' matching workflow definition.
procedures: Construct URL with owner, repo, workflow_id.
Include JSON body with 'ref' and optional 'inputs'.
context_pack.summary: API endpoint to trigger a GitHub Actions workflow dispatch for a specified workflow in a repository.
